claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.
  - Blake is 10 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 10 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To start, I need to understand your situation. 

**First question: Do you have a child or children you're claiming for?** If yes, please tell me:
- How many children you have
- Their name(s) 
- Their age(s)

This will help me determine which eligibility path applies to your situation. Child Benefit rules differ based on whether a child is:
- Under 16 years old
- Between 16-19 years old (with education/training requirements)
- 20 or over (generally not eligible)

Please share these details about your child or children.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! Yes, I'm looking to claim for my two children. I have Alex who is 5 years old, and Blake who is 10 years old.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! Thank you for that information. You have:
- **Alex** (5 years old)
- **Blake** (10 years old)

Both children are under 16, which means they can potentially qualify for Child Benefit. Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now, the next question:

**Are both Alex and Blake working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?**

Given their ages (5 and 10), they're likely not in paid employment, but I need to confirm this to proceed with the assessment.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Oh no, neither of them work at all! They're just kids - Alex is only 5 and Blake is 10. They're both in school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's perfect - thank you for confirming! Since they're both in school and not working, we can proceed.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are Alex and Blake NOT receiving any benefits in their own right?**

This means benefits like Employment and Support Allowance (ESA) or Universal Credit that would be claimed in the child's own name (not yours as the parent). 

For most children aged 5 and 10 in school, this wouldn't apply, but I need to confirm they're not receiving these types of benefits.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, they don't receive any benefits in their own names. They're just regular kids - no benefits for either of them.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Excellent! Let me continue with the assessment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Next question:

**Are Alex and Blake NOT in any apprenticeships?**

Given their ages (5 and 10) and that they're in regular school, I expect the answer is that they're not in apprenticeships. But I need to confirm this to proceed.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, definitely not! They're way too young for apprenticeships. Alex is only 5 and Blake is 10 - they're just in primary school.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Absolutely right! They're far too young for apprenticeships. Let me continue.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Now I need to ask about **your** residency status:

**Do you normally live in the UK?**

This is one of the key requirements for claiming Child Benefit. The rules also allow for certain other situations such as:
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme
- Being a Crown servant living abroad
- Living in certain qualifying countries

But for most people, the simple answer is: do you normally live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I don't live in the UK. I'm living abroad at the moment.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since you're living abroad, I need to ask some follow-up questions to see if you might still qualify under special circumstances.

**Which of the following applies to you?**

1. **Are you a Crown servant** (e.g., working for the UK government, diplomatic service, armed forces) living abroad?

2. **Are you living in a qualifying country** where UK Child Benefit can still be claimed?

3. **Do you have settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme** and meet certain conditions (working, studying, or have sufficient resources)?

4. **None of the above** - you're simply living abroad for other reasons?

Please let me know which situation applies to you, as this will determine whether you can still claim Child Benefit while living outside the UK.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: None of those apply to me. I'm just living abroad for other reasons - not a Crown servant, don't have any special EU status or anything like that.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for clarifying. Unfortunately, this means you don't meet the UK residency requirements for Child Benefit.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Assessment Complete: **NOT ELIGIBLE**

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit** for Alex or Blake.

### Summary:

**For Alex (age 5):**
- ✅ Meets child criteria (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in apprenticeship)
- ❌ **You don't meet UK residency requirements**

**For Blake (age 10):**
- ✅ Meets child criteria (under 16, not working, not receiving benefits, not in apprenticeship)
- ❌ **You don't meet UK residency requirements**

### Why you're not eligible:

To claim Child Benefit, you must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances such as:
- Being a Crown servant working abroad
- Having settled or pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme (with additional qualifying criteria like working, studying, or having sufficient resources)
- Living in certain qualifying countries

Since you're living abroad without any of these special circumstances, you cannot claim Child Benefit at this time.

### What you could do:

- **If you move back to the UK** and become normally resident there, you could claim Child Benefit at that time
- **If your circumstances change** (e.g., you become a Crown servant or gain qualifying immigration status), you may become eligible

If you have any questions about this decision or your circumstances change, you can contact HMRC's Child Benefit office for further guidance.

reasonings: Alex (age 5) meets the child-related criteria as they are under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements necessary to claim Child Benefit. The claimant is living abroad and does not qualify under any special circumstances (Crown servant, EU Settlement Scheme status, or living in qualifying countries).

Blake (age 10) meets the child-related criteria as they are under 16, not working, not receiving benefits in their own right, and not in an apprenticeship. However, the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements necessary to claim Child Benefit. The claimant is living abroad and does not qualify under any special circumstances (Crown servant, EU Settlement Scheme status, or living in qualifying countries).
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children (Alex, age 5, and Blake, age 10) who both meet all child-related eligibility criteria for Child Benefit. However, the claim cannot proceed because the claimant does not meet the UK residency requirements. To claim Child Benefit, the claimant must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances such as being a Crown servant abroad, having settled/pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme with qualifying criteria, or living in certain qualifying countries. As the claimant is living abroad without any of these special circumstances, they are ineligible to claim Child Benefit for either child.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.question: Is the child working less than 24 hours per week (or not in paid work)?
response.help_text: Children aged 16+ working 24+ hours per week and not in approved education/training are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT receiving certain benefits in their own right (e.g., ESA, Universal Credit)?
response.help_text: Children receiving Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit in their own right are not eligible

response.question: Is the child NOT in a disqualifying apprenticeship?
response.help_text: Regional rules apply: English apprenticeships (all types) disqualify. Scottish Modern Apprenticeships disqualify. Welsh Foundation Apprenticeships are APPROVED. Other Welsh apprenticeships disqualify.

response.question: Does the claimant meet UK residency requirements?
response.help_text: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances

paths.requirement: Claimant normally lives in the UK

paths.description: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
paths.requirement: Has settled status under EU Settlement Scheme

criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Working and earning/expect to earn above primary National Insurance threshold for 3 continuous months

paths.description: Pre-settled status and actively seeking work
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Looking for work (can claim as jobseeker for up to 91 days)
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Has sufficient resources to support self and family financially
NOT claiming Income Support, income-based JSA, Pension Credit, or Universal Credit
Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Studying and has sufficient resources to support self financially

paths.description: Pre-settled status as family member of EEA/Swiss national
criteria: Has pre-settled status under EU Settlement Scheme
Is family member of EEA or Swiss national with right to reside
One of: spouse/civil partner, dependent child/grandchild under 21, dependent parent/grandparent

paths.requirement: Living abroad as Crown servant or in certain qualifying countries

response.evaluation_logic: If ANY path is satisfied, proceed to responsibility check

response.reason: Does not meet UK residency requirements
response.guidance: Must normally live in the UK or meet special circumstances (settled status, pre-settled status with qualifying criteria, Crown servant, or living in certain countries). If you have pre-settled status, you may need to be working, studying, or have sufficient resources.
